must not: line breaks must not be included
must: the entire line containing base 64 encoding must be read before it can be processed

Mandatory: should require all commands be protected
should use the same level of protection
should refuse to accept a non-encrypted PASS command
Recommended: recommendations which an implementation should implement
recommended that an implementation provide all commands which can be implemented
Not Recommended: use of CCC is not recommended

8. Declarative specifications.data:
{
    "must be formatted": "must be formatted as described in section 9",
    "is defined as": "is defined as a string",
    "is defined as": "is defined as a string that must be formatted as described in section 9",
    "is defined as": "is defined as C, S, E, or P",
    "is defined as": "is defined as any decimal integer from 1 to (2^32)-1"
}

Mandatory: must use integrity protection on the control stream
is always required to specify the identity
must be transmitted with integrity protection
must be transmitted with integrity protection
Prohibited: may not actually include any authentication at all
refuse to do file transfers without integrity protection

must not be included: The square brackets are not to be included in the reply
must be: The exact representation of the challenge should be chosen by the mechanism to be sensible to the human user of the system
then base 64 encoded in order into a separate line of the multiline reply
Telnet end-of-line codes must appear in the plaintext of the encoded reply
The multiline reply must be formatted more strictly than the continuation specification in RFC 959
each line before the last must be formed by the reply code
followed by a base 64 encoded fragment of the reply
may only be: A protected reply may only be sent if a security data exchange has succeeded
cannot: How failures decoding or verifying replies are handled is implementation-specific
must: There need not be any correspondence between the line breaks in the plaintext reply and the encoded reply

Prohibited: must not be willing to accept both
